Appendix: Acknowledgement of Generative AI Use

Author

Sinping CHAN

I acknowledge the use of [1] Gemini 3 Flash [2] (https://gemini.google.com/) and [1] Claude Sonnet 4.6 [2] (https://claude.ai/) to [3] generate ideas, structure suggestions, draft initial outlines and rewrite text, and provide code enhancements that were [3] included within my final assessment in modified form. I entered the following prompts on 9 April 2026:

  • [3] Structure the “Dataset Introduction” and “Dataset Description” sections to ensure a professional distinction between business context and technical data details. [4] I edited the drafts to ensure the specific dates (Sept 2024 – Feb 2026) and customer segments accurately reflected my dataset.

  • [3] Refine the academic style, English phrasing, and professional terminology throughout the assignment, including the Introduction, Dataset Description, and Results sections, ensuring a “highly skewed distribution” was correctly explained. [4] I reviewed and modified the suggested text to ensure it accurately reflected my specific findings and maintained a consistent voice.

  • [3] Develop code for data visualizations (Figures), specifically the LOESS smoothing plot, and provide advice on professional academic formatting such as removing internal titles and using Quarto cross-referencing. [4] I confirmed that the visualization parameters accurately represented the trends and distributions found in my data.

  • [3] Generate R code and formatting for all tables, including the primary “ROI Summary Table” with conditional formatting (red text for negative ROI) and the granular “ROI Check” mini-table. [4] I cross-checked the outputted summary statistics against the raw data to ensure accuracy.

  • [3] Troubleshoot technical issues regarding Quarto rendering (resolving “object not found” errors), cross-referencing, and Git connectivity within RStudio.

  • [3] Documentation of AI Interactions: Due to technical limitations in the chat platform, the full log of my interactions is documented in three parts within the supplementary files:

    1. Part 1: Key interactions regarding data cleaning and initial visualization with Gemini are provided as screenshots in the following pages of this appendix.

    2. Part 2: Subsequent full conversation logs with Gemini are documented in the separate file: Gemini_chat.docx

    3. Part 3: Full conversation logs and specific prompt iterations with Claude can be found at the following link: [HERE]
